unit_15 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_15

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 357 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Text Completion
Biologists have little (i)______ drawing the link between the success of humanity and human (ii)______. Indeed, many biologists claim that this attribute, the ability to (iii)______, or, to put it more sharply, to make individuals subordinate their self-interest to the needs of the group, lies at the root of human achievement.Biologists have little (i)______ drawing the link between the success of humanity and human (ii)______. Indeed, many biologists claim that this attribute, the ability to (iii)______, or, to put it more sharply, to make individuals subordinate their self-interest to the needs of the group, lies at the root of human achievement.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　compunction about; sociability; cooperate
consensusdrawlieattribute
consensus: standard unit headword | draw: standard unit headword | lie: standard unit headword | attribute: standard unit headword
#2 Text Completion
The journey to (i)______ , when it starts from a vantage as (ii)______ as Dunsany’s, is often as (iii)______ as the path to glory. How did a writer of such talent and renown wind up nearly forgotten?The journey to (i)______ , when it starts from a vantage as (ii)______ as Dunsany’s, is often as (iii)______ as the path to glory. How did a writer of such talent and renown wind up nearly forgotten?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　obscurity; eminent; unfathomable
eminentreactionarymundanecyclical
eminent: standard unit headword | reactionary: standard unit headword | mundane: standard unit headword | cyclical: standard unit headword
#3 Text Completion
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
modelinitiallycircumvent
model: standard unit headword | initially: standard unit headword | circumvent: standard unit headword
#4 Sentence Equivalence
The company is so old-fashioned and opposed to innovation that it can seem downright ______.The company is so old-fashioned and opposed to innovation that it can seem downright ______.
答案：A, C　antediluvian; archaic
fashionold-fashioned
fashion: standard unit headword | old-fashioned: standard unit headword

答案：E　obtrude into
minimizeillusion
minimize: standard unit headword | illusion: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　temptation; occlude

#7 Sentence Equivalence
The environmental threat posed by the New Zealand mud snail, an invasive species, is ______ by the snail’s small size, which facilitates its spread across watersheds through human activity.The environmental threat posed by the New Zealand mud snail, an invasive species, is ______ by the snail’s small size, which facilitates its spread across watersheds through human activity.
答案：C, F　exacerbated; aggravated
extenuateinitiate
extenuate: standard unit headword | initiate: standard unit headword
#8 Text Completion
Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)______ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)______ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)______. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)______ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)______ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)______. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　operate outside of; active; report
controlorientation
control: standard unit headword | orientation: standard unit headword
#9 TC TRIPLE
It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) rather than (ii) . That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (ii) their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) rather than (ii) . That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (ii) their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　exaggerated expectations; realism; preclude
markedminimizepreclude
marked: standard unit headword | minimize: standard unit headword | preclude: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　accelerated; rebuffed
interminablegrant

Doubts and worries beset technical and scientific specialists, as well as the public at large. I do not consider such worries (ii)______, though they are often based on intuitive feeling rather than on strictly logical arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　uncritical joy; unfounded
foreordainsacrosanct

The concept of increasing complexity of organisms has ______ history among evolutionary biologists, and yet many laypeople would unhesitatingly say that the pattern applies to the history of life on Earth.The concept of increasing complexity of organisms has ______ history among evolutionary biologists, and yet many laypeople would unhesitatingly say that the pattern applies to the history of life on Earth.
答案：D, F　a contentious; a fraught
fraughtlaypeople
fraught: standard unit headword | laypeople: standard unit headword

Reviews written by music critic and composer Stephenson were hardly (i)______: musicians who performed his music could count on sympathetic coverage, while those who ignored him were held to (ii)______ standards.Reviews written by music critic and composer Stephenson were hardly (i)______: musicians who performed his music could count on sympathetic coverage, while those who ignored him were held to (ii)______ standards.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　disinterested; exacting
minimallucid
minimal: standard unit headword | lucid: standard unit headword
#14 Text Completion
Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)______ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)______, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)______ of critical thought.Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)______ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)______, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)______ of critical thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　an impediment to; hasty; stagnation
dilatorymarker
dilatory: standard unit headword | marker: standard unit headword
#15 Text Completion
Unlike the elected branches of the United States government, where making personal connection with citizens is (i)______ and almost (ii)______ political efficacy, the United States Supreme Court continues to maintain that its members should communicate with the public almost exclusively through formal opinions—and even then through ceremonial rituals that date back to the nineteenth century.Unlike the elected branches of the United States government, where making personal connection with citizens is (i)______ and almost (ii)______ political efficacy, the United States Supreme Court continues to maintain that its members should communicate with the public almost exclusively through formal opinions—and even then through ceremonial rituals that date back to the nineteenth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　rampant; a requirement for

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　amenable to refutation; upended; persistent

Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　empowering; surrendering to; glean precisely what you want from it

Blank 3

The defining characteristic of English company law at that time was its extreme degree of permissiveness: laissez-faire ruled in Parliament, caveat emptor in the courts, creating the ideal breeding ground for fraud and dishonesty. Yet these were the years when London became the financial center of the world and established its status as a (iii) ____________ place to do business.Nineteenth-century England presents something of (i) __________ , at least to those who believe that (ii) ___________ best promotes business ethics. The defining characteristic of English company law at that time was its extreme degree of permissiveness: laissez-faire ruled in Parliament, caveat emptor in the courts, creating the ideal breeding ground for fraud and dishonesty. Yet these were the years when London became the financial center of the world and established its status as a (iii) ____________ place to do business.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　an enigma; regulation; comparatively safe
enigma
enigma: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude

Blank 2

答案：A　incontrovertible

答案：E　disappearance
magnitude
magnitude: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　tantamount to; commensurate with

答案：B, E　shackle; stifle
weird
weird: standard unit headword

答案：C, F　stimulated; spurred

答案：C, E　spirited; vigorous

#47 Sentence Equivalence
Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can ______ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can ______ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.
答案：A, F　hamper; impede
nomenclature
nomenclature: standard unit headword
#48 Sentence Equivalence

答案：B, C　immaterial; inconsequential
prophylactic
prophylactic: standard unit headword

It may not seem like a big deal for produce distributors to mix together soybeans from different farms, but a growing number of buyers are willing to pay a premium for soybeans the ______ of which is known: they like to know exactly where their food came from.It may not seem like a big deal for produce distributors to mix together soybeans from different farms, but a growing number of buyers are willing to pay a premium for soybeans the ______ of which is known: they like to know exactly where their food came from.
答案：D, F　origin; provenance
provenance
provenance: standard unit headword
#50 Text Completion
Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of ______, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of ______, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.
答案：A　synthesis
drawperforce

答案：A, C　malfeasance; fraudulence
hysteria
hysteria: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　plummeted; decline
plummet

答案：A, E　prodigious; voracious
omnivorous
omnivorous: standard unit headword
#54 Text Completion
The governor might conceivably find a genuine resolution to the budgetary dilemma, but she may be tempted to engage in a deception: a ______ exercise in fiscal prudence.The governor might conceivably find a genuine resolution to the budgetary dilemma, but she may be tempted to engage in a deception: a ______ exercise in fiscal prudence.
答案：C　specious
tempt
tempt: standard unit headword

The town’s air was consistently ______: depending on the breeze, one might be greeted with the sour effluvia of twenty breweries, choking fumes from the coal tar factory, or brackish smells from the nearby river.The town’s air was consistently ______: depending on the breeze, one might be greeted with the sour effluvia of twenty breweries, choking fumes from the coal tar factory, or brackish smells from the nearby river.
答案：A, E　malodorous; noisome

Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)______ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the review’s task is more (ii)______: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)______ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)______ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the review’s task is more (ii)______: the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)______ the useless and tedious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from

#57 Text Completion
To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage

initiative: standard unit headword

Common sense tells us some people are more (i)______ than others. The claim that these differences are (ii)______, or that deep down, everybody acts only to further their own interests, (iii)______ observations and deep-seated human practices of moral evaluation.Common sense tells us some people are more (i)______ than others. The claim that these differences are (ii)______, or that deep down, everybody acts only to further their own interests, (iii)______ observations and deep-seated human practices of moral evaluation.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　altruistic; illusory; contradicts

答案：D, E　solidarity; camaraderie

答案：B　stalwart

答案：E　pretext for

To a mathematician, it means little that the equations (iii)_______. This layout poses many dangers for the serious historian, not the least of which being the (ii)______ reception that academics—motivated partly by (iii)______ but also by genuine concern over scholarly standards—generally reserve for books apparently aimed at the popular market.This book cannot be evaluated properly without examining the author’s choice of format, which is the (i)______ of the format of standard academic works; here the photographs take center stage, with the text playing only a supporting role. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) ____________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) ____________.The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) ____________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) ____________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. Blank 2
Blank 3

Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) ____________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) ____________ as time passes.The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) ____________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) ____________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) ____________ as time passes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　dynamic; sound; misleading

Throughout United States history there has been (iii)______ seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i)______, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii)______ human spaceflight. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (ii) by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as(i) as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (ii) by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.

Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism

Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom (ii) __________ their literary models. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound
model
model: standard unit headword
#105 TC SINGLE
Central control of the country's power grid is difficult because of the __________ of the electricity industry-it is made up of hundreds of local networks with separate owners.Central control of the country's power grid is difficult because of the __________ of the electricity industry-it is made up of hundreds of local networks with separate owners.
答案：E　balkanization
control
control: standard unit headword
#106 TC DOUBLE
The comments are (i)________ to be sure, meant to suggest new lines of research rather than to deal with the subject in (ii)________ manner.The comments are (i)________ to be sure, meant to suggest new lines of research rather than to deal with the subject in (ii)________ manner.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　sketchy; an exhaustive
exhaustive
exhaustive: standard unit headword
#107 SE
Because it has usually been impossible to _________ exotic species once they have become established, it is prudent to minimize the introduction of such species that have a substantial probability of unwanted impacts.Because it has usually been impossible to _________ exotic species once they have become established, it is prudent to minimize the introduction of such species that have a substantial probability of unwanted impacts.
答案：C, E　eradicate; extirpate
minimize
minimize: standard unit headword
#108 TC TRIPLE
Models of the interior evolution of Venus suggest that the planet (i) __________ periods of relative (ii) __________ and periods of instability and rapid surface overturn. In other words, Venus may "freak out" occasionally, getting rid of its internal heat in great planetwide (iii) __________ of activity, rather than in the steadier cycling of lithospheric plates that occurs on Earth.Models of the interior evolution of Venus suggest that the planet (i) __________ periods of relative (ii) __________ and periods of instability and rapid surface overturn. In other words, Venus may "freak out" occasionally, getting rid of its internal heat in great planetwide (iii) __________ of activity, rather than in the steadier cycling of lithospheric plates that occurs on Earth.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　oscillates between; quiescence; paroxysms
model
model: standard unit headword
#109 TC SINGLE
The paintings of the 1960s Photo-Realists were meticulously rendered by artists who eschewed abstraction for realistic representation and thus achieved a precisely detailed, impersonal __________.The paintings of the 1960s Photo-Realists were meticulously rendered by artists who eschewed abstraction for realistic representation and thus achieved a precisely detailed, impersonal __________.
答案：A　verisimilitude
verisimilitude
verisimilitude: standard unit headword
#110 SE
It may be fairly easy to criticize Tatsumi's comic book on a drawing-by-drawing or line-by-line basis, but one ought not make that mistake, for the book's pleasures are __________ .It may be fairly easy to criticize Tatsumi's comic book on a drawing-by-drawing or line-by-line basis, but one ought not make that mistake, for the book's pleasures are __________ .
答案：C, F　cumulative; additive
draw
draw: standard unit headword
#111 TC DOUBLE
Although fears of a dark, robot-controlled future are evident in the popular imagination, some scientists are more (i)________ about the impact that artificial intelligence will have in real life. They share a generally (ii)________ vision of a future in which robots interact cooperatively with people in a wide variety of settings.Although fears of a dark, robot-controlled future are evident in the popular imagination, some scientists are more (i)________ about the impact that artificial intelligence will have in real life. They share a generally (ii)________ vision of a future in which robots interact cooperatively with people in a wide variety of settings.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　sanguine; propitious
control
control: standard unit headword
#112 TC TRIPLE
According to Merrin, many law school curricula are (i)_______ the challenge of fostering an adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with, core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a (ii)_______ ethics, thus creating an (iii)_______ audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.According to Merrin, many law school curricula are (i)_______ the challenge of fostering an adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with, core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a (ii)_______ ethics, thus creating an (iii)_______ audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　unequal to; cynical sense of; unfavorable
cynical
cynical: standard unit headword
#113 TC SINGLE
The efficacy of a placebo may not ___________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.The efficacy of a placebo may not ___________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.
答案：C　require
precludecircumvent
preclude: standard unit headword | circumvent: standard unit headword
#114 SE
Such is the strength of randomized controlled trials that the debate among medical researchers is now centered on whether there are any situations where such a trial is________.Such is the strength of randomized controlled trials that the debate among medical researchers is now centered on whether there are any situations where such a trial is________.
答案：B, C　unwarranted; inappropriate
control
control: standard unit headword
#115 TC DOUBLE
In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle’s corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i)_______ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii)_______ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle’s corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i)_______ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii)_______ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　unique; can be traced to
attribute
attribute: standard unit headword
#116 Sentence Equivalence
In ways large and small, millions of people are taking active steps to ______ the medical mainstream, whether by taking herbal remedies for disease or by placing their hopes for a cure in alternative treatment.In ways large and small, millions of people are taking active steps to ______ the medical mainstream, whether by taking herbal remedies for disease or by placing their hopes for a cure in alternative treatment.
答案：B, E　sidestep; circumvent
circumvent
circumvent: standard unit headword
#117 TC TRIPLE
The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　difficult; absurd; explained
grant
grant: standard unit headword

Refusing to speak plainly, the candidate tested her audience's patience with her ___________ speech.Refusing to speak plainly, the candidate tested her audience's patience with her ___________ speech.
答案：B　fustian
lucid
lucid: standard unit headword
#119 TC DOUBLE
There is little question that in primates there is (i)________ between monogamous mating partnerships and extensive male care of infants. Nevertheless, that does not mean male care is (ii)________ situations in which females mate with more than one male; studies of savanna baboons show that male care certainly can occur in nonmonogamous systems.There is little question that in primates there is (i)________ between monogamous mating partnerships and extensive male care of infants. Nevertheless, that does not mean male care is (ii)________ situations in which females mate with more than one male; studies of savanna baboons show that male care certainly can occur in nonmonogamous systems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　an association; precluded by
preclude
preclude: standard unit headword
#120 SE
It is often taken for granted by scholars that Emily Dickinson is __________ poet: her poems have a peculiar quality that makes understanding seem beyond the reader's ken.It is often taken for granted by scholars that Emily Dickinson is __________ poet: her poems have a peculiar quality that makes understanding seem beyond the reader's ken.
答案：D, F　a difficult; an enigmatic
grant
grant: standard unit headword

答案：A　an inscrutable

答案：A, C　encyclopedic; exhaustive

#123 TC TRIPLE
Reading Frankfurt's book. I worried that in the absence of specifie examples or nuaneed analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.Reading Frankfurt's book. I worried that in the absence of specifie examples or nuaneed analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　enthusiastically vilified; scapegoat; horror
model
model: standard unit headword

The approach initially adopted by historians of women, which revolved around the __________ of women in earlier periods, significantly enriched historical knowledge but inadvertently limited the feminist historian's task to simply recovering previously ignored facts.The approach initially adopted by historians of women, which revolved around the __________ of women in earlier periods, significantly enriched historical knowledge but inadvertently limited the feminist historian's task to simply recovering previously ignored facts.
答案：A, E　visibility; recognition
initially
initially: standard unit headword
#125 TC DOUBLE
Despite Freud's admitted agnosticism about whether consciousness has a neurological basis, the question was (i) __________ to his model of the mind. Indeed, placing consciousness in a general neurophysiological schema of the mind (ii) __________ Freud's entire conceptual understanding of the psyche.Despite Freud's admitted agnosticism about whether consciousness has a neurological basis, the question was (i) __________ to his model of the mind. Indeed, placing consciousness in a general neurophysiological schema of the mind (ii) __________ Freud's entire conceptual understanding of the psyche.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　critical; oriented

Although its gray text blocks and black-and-white illustrations give it a sober mien, this one-stop resource can take the place of a dozen less ______ texts.Although its gray text blocks and black-and-white illustrations give it a sober mien, this one-stop resource can take the place of a dozen less ______ texts.
答案：A, F　exhaustive; comprehensive

答案：C　efficient

Blank 1

Blank 3

Blank 2

Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. Fashion designer Elsa Schiaparelli was just nineteen years old when Futurism erupted, but she later heeded that violent, belligerent art movement's call in the (i) __________ of her broad-shouldered suits, the rawness of her furs and embroidery, and her (ii) __________ attitude, which her contemporaries described as "hard chic," toward any simpering or mincing in fashion.Fashion designer Elsa Schiaparelli was just nineteen years old when Futurism erupted, but she later heeded that violent, belligerent art movement's call in the (i) __________ of her broad-shouldered suits, the rawness of her furs and embroidery, and her (ii) __________ attitude, which her contemporaries described as "hard chic," toward any simpering or mincing in fashion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　swagger; pugnacious
fashion

答案：D　paradoxical

Blank 2

答案：E, F　yield; engender

答案：B, D　abjure; repudiate

Blank 2

Blank 2

lie: standard unit headword | initially: standard unit headword

ForeachlankselectoneentyfomthecorrespondincolumofehoicesFabanksinthewaythat best completes the text In the mid-1990s,researchers were avware that individuals and subpopulationsvaryin thei susceptiblties to evironmentalpolutants.However ervironrentalhealth scientists,hoping to identify qeneral trends, tended to view these variations as (i) and used methods,in both human and animal studies, to (ii) Blank(iForeachlankselectoneentyfomthecorrespondincolumofehoicesFabanksinthewaythat best completes the text In the mid-1990s,researchers were avware that individuals and subpopulationsvaryin thei susceptiblties to evironmentalpolutants.However ervironrentalhealth scientists,hoping to identify qeneral trends, tended to view these variations as (i) and used methods,in both human and animal studies, to (ii) Blank(i
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　distractions; minimize their effect

答案：C, D　meager; paltry

draw: standard unit headword

答案：A, E　cooperate; collaborate

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　sterling reputation of; exhaustive
exhaustive

Progressive and reactionary populist movements are not necessarily ______: each may, and usually does, possess the features of the other.Progressive and reactionary populist movements are not necessarily ______: each may, and usually does, possess the features of the other.
答案：A, D　dichotomous; contradictory
reactionary
reactionary: standard unit headword
#164 SE
The chancellor once openly admired the relatively laissez-faire economic recipes of Britain and the United States but now defends the German way, in which she sees the free market’s sometimes destructive vicissitudes being _______ by consensus-building politics and a generous welfare state.The chancellor once openly admired the relatively laissez-faire economic recipes of Britain and the United States but now defends the German way, in which she sees the free market’s sometimes destructive vicissitudes being _______ by consensus-building politics and a generous welfare state.
答案：A, E　tempered; mitigated
consensus
consensus: standard unit headword

Early animals were primarily decomposers, feeding off detritus left by plants. However, as the Carboniferous period progressed, insects began to eat the plants themselves, a development that would have (i) natural selection of plants with (ii) plant tissues and perhaps early chemical defense mechanisms.Early animals were primarily decomposers, feeding off detritus left by plants. However, as the Carboniferous period progressed, insects began to eat the plants themselves, a development that would have (i) natural selection of plants with (ii) plant tissues and perhaps early chemical defense mechanisms.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　promoted; tougher
preclude
preclude: standard unit headword

(ii)______, the text makes it clear that the author’s examination of these sources has been similarly (iii)______.Fifty pages of footnotes, some of them presenting quite lengthy bibliographies, suggest that very few pertinent sources on the Black Arts movement in literature have (i)______ Thompson’s search. (ii)______, the text makes it clear that the author’s examination of these sources has been similarly (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　eluded; Moreover; exhaustive
exhaustive
exhaustive: standard unit headword

What they see in Jimenez is the one candidate capable of decisive leadership, in stark contrast to Diza, whose term in office has been marred by ______.What they see in Jimenez is the one candidate capable of decisive leadership, in stark contrast to Diza, whose term in office has been marred by ______.
答案：C, D　irresolution; vacillation

mar: standard unit headword
#168 SE
The reliable production of usable energy through the controlled fusion of hydrogen atoms into helium remains an elusive dream: even the most ardent efforts to achieve it have thus far been __________.The reliable production of usable energy through the controlled fusion of hydrogen atoms into helium remains an elusive dream: even the most ardent efforts to achieve it have thus far been __________.
答案：C, F　thwarted; frustrated
control
control: standard unit headword
#169 Text Completion
The space travels described in science fiction stories always used to be epic adventures, in comparison to which current journeys in space seem quite ______.The space travels described in science fiction stories always used to be epic adventures, in comparison to which current journeys in space seem quite ______.
答案：A　mundane
mundane
mundane: standard unit headword

Blank 2

答案：C, E　hailed; acclaimed
model
model: standard unit headword
#172 Text Completion
With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)______. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)______ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)______ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)______. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)______ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)______ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane
mundane
mundane: standard unit headword
#173 SE
It is true that propagandists spout misinformation to serve their own ends, but if our minds were __________ such garbage, the lies would die out quickly.It is true that propagandists spout misinformation to serve their own ends, but if our minds were __________ such garbage, the lies would die out quickly.
答案：A, C　inhospitable to; unreceptive to

lie: standard unit headword
#174 TC DOUBLE
Biographer John Richardson cautions that Picasso's relationship with Surrealism is easily (i) __________. He stresses that, unlike the Surrealists', Picasso's art was always rooted in some concrete reality, no matter how unreal the imagery may seem; even his most (ii) __________ pictures are representations of the people in his life.Biographer John Richardson cautions that Picasso's relationship with Surrealism is easily (i) __________. He stresses that, unlike the Surrealists', Picasso's art was always rooted in some concrete reality, no matter how unreal the imagery may seem; even his most (ii) __________ pictures are representations of the people in his life.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　overstated; hallucinatory
mundane
mundane: standard unit headword

Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to ______ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to ______ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.
答案：C, F　champion; defend
orientation
orientation: standard unit headword

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　centralizing; raise doubts about; preclude

答案：A, C　contingent; circumstantial

Blank 2

答案：C, F　detracting; deprecatory

答案：A, D　judicious; sensible

Blank 2

答案：C, F　obsolete; outmoded

答案：A, F　unwise; misguided

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　emulate; anachronistic

答案：D, E　alarm; unsettle

Blank 2

答案：A, F　pugnacious; truculent

Indeed, many critics (ii)______ her because of her willingness to (iii)______.The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)______. The fact that science dealt in statistics and numbers, that its logic was probabilistic, meant that the domain of moral questions (ii)______ it: moral questions, for Tagore, required certainties, not probabilities.

答案：A, E　lacking; wanting

答案：A, F　accession to; acquiescence to

Hugh Trevor-Roper’s series of essays on the Puritan Revolution are considered to be the _______ of the genre: though these essays have exerted a welcome influence on the work of many subsequent historians, their quality has never been equaled.Hugh Trevor-Roper’s series of essays on the Puritan Revolution are considered to be the _______ of the genre: though these essays have exerted a welcome influence on the work of many subsequent historians, their quality has never been equaled.
答案：B, D　apogee; acme

#216 Text Completion
Many creative photographers were delighted to find in instant photography a mode that encouraged them to stop viewing photography as ______ and start viewing it as something they could handle with spontaneity, even derision.Many creative photographers were delighted to find in instant photography a mode that encouraged them to stop viewing photography as ______ and start viewing it as something they could handle with spontaneity, even derision.
答案：A　sacrosanct
sacrosanct

答案：B, E　fosters; adopts

答案：A, C　hackneyed; pedestrian
fashionable
fashionable: standard unit headword
#219 Text Completion
The lack of (i)______ the poetry of the postwar decades has led not, as one might have expected, to (ii)______ poetry, but to a curious closure in which all poets and forms of poetry are (iii)______ and alone, not even respected establishment poets.The lack of (i)______ the poetry of the postwar decades has led not, as one might have expected, to (ii)______ poetry, but to a curious closure in which all poets and forms of poetry are (iii)______ and alone, not even respected establishment poets.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　consensus about; pluralistic debate on; marginalized

#220 Sentence Equivalence
There are many ways in which rat brains and human brains are ______; indeed, rat brains are often used as generalized models for all mammalian brains, including our own.There are many ways in which rat brains and human brains are ______; indeed, rat brains are often used as generalized models for all mammalian brains, including our own.
答案：B, D　analogous; comparable
model
model: standard unit headword
#221 SE
The artist reworked certain of his etchings in order to make the scenes they depict more logical, yet, paradoxically, the pictorial space seems to become more __________ the more realistically it is defined.The artist reworked certain of his etchings in order to make the scenes they depict more logical, yet, paradoxically, the pictorial space seems to become more __________ the more realistically it is defined.
答案：D, F　fanciful; chimerical
lucid
lucid: standard unit headword
#222 Text Completion
Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)______ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer’s task is more (ii)______ : the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)______ the uselessBoth very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)______ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer’s task is more (ii)______ : the author’s useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)______ the useless
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from
sift
sift: standard unit headword
#223 Sentence Equivalence
For all the ______ the new CEO has received from the press recently, her staff have a decidedly less rosy view of her.For all the ______ the new CEO has received from the press recently, her staff have a decidedly less rosy view of her.
答案：A, B　encomiums; tributes
press
press: standard unit headword
#224 SE
At the turn of the twentieth century, the status of dinosaurs changed dramatically in the United States; fascination with them, previously __________ passion, became widespread.At the turn of the twentieth century, the status of dinosaurs changed dramatically in the United States; fascination with them, previously __________ passion, became widespread.
答案：A, F　an esoteric; an arcane
mundane
mundane: standard unit headword
#225 Text Completion
Some species camouflage patterns may be ______, granting those species a higher chance of survival in a heterogeneous environment.Some species camouflage patterns may be ______, granting those species a higher chance of survival in a heterogeneous environment.
答案：A　plastic
grant
grant: standard unit headword
#226 Sentence Equivalence
Although one can adduce myriad of examples of ecosystem disruption by nonindigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survived in fact appear to have quite ______ effects on the ecosystem they have invaded.Although one can adduce myriad of examples of ecosystem disruption by nonindigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survived in fact appear to have quite ______ effects on the ecosystem they have invaded.
答案：C, E　minimal; trifling

marked: standard unit headword | minimal: standard unit headword

The simplicity of Ann Hamilton's sculpture, a single drop of water snaking its way down a vast white wall, __________ the elaborate technical apparatus hidden beneath its surface.The simplicity of Ann Hamilton's sculpture, a single drop of water snaking its way down a vast white wall, __________ the elaborate technical apparatus hidden beneath its surface.
答案：C, D　belied; contradicted
minimize
minimize: standard unit headword
#228 Text Completion
We often regard natural phenomena like rainfall as mysterious and unpredictable; although for short time spans and particular places they appear so, in fact on a truly global scale, nature has been a model of ______.We often regard natural phenomena like rainfall as mysterious and unpredictable; although for short time spans and particular places they appear so, in fact on a truly global scale, nature has been a model of ______.
答案：A　reliability
model
model: standard unit headword

答案：C, D　draw; elicit

答案：D, F　coercive; imperious

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　derivative; remarkably pioneering

答案：B, E　anathema to; repellent to

答案：A　superficial

Pioneering medical research scientists’ (i)______ claims regarding the (ii)______ new researches make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)______. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.Pioneering medical research scientists’ (i)______ claims regarding the (ii)______ new researches make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)______. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　inflated; potential benefits of; oversell

Throughout United States history there has been (iii)______ —seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i)______ , yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii)______ human spaceflight. Blank 2
Blank 3

Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)______ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)______, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)______ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)______ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)______, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.

Blank 2
Blank 3

Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii)______ by the frogs.The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poison called batrachotoxins. But the (i)______ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii)______ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Blank 2

Blank 3

Blank 2
Blank 3

#276 Text Completion

Blank 1

答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating

Although counterintuitive, the finding is (ii)______ some past research on atrazine and studies showing that other hormonally active compounds are most damaging at trace concentrations.In aquatic environments, the herbicide atrazine is more likely to (i)______ developing amphibians when it is highly diluted than when it is much more concentrated, a new study suggests. Although counterintuitive, the finding is (ii)______ some past research on atrazine and studies showing that other hormonally active compounds are most damaging at trace concentrations.

答案：B, E　kill; consistent with

The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______ , a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to use forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______ : the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______ , a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to use forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill

答案：B, E, H　scientific; admitted; necessary characteristic
